what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG outline icon depicting an open folder, using inherited currentColor strokes and no fill.
observations: The root svg declares the SVG namespace and a viewBox of 0 0 24 24.
The icon uses fill="none" and stroke="currentColor".
Stroke styling is inline: stroke-width="1.5", stroke-linecap="round", and stroke-linejoin="round".
The drawing is made from two path elements: one for the open folder body and one for the folder tab/top shape.
standouts: The icon is entirely stroke-based with no filled shapes.
The geometry is compactly encoded in two path elements.
conventions: Uses rounded stroke caps and joins for an outline icon style.
Contains no metadata, title, role, class, script, or embedded styling.
concerns: No accessible title or aria labeling is included in the SVG itself.
notes: A compact inline SVG asset for an open-folder outline icon.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming component instead of inside this SVG?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename/path indicates an open-folder icon, and the SVG path geometry depicts an open folder, so the apparent purpose matches the content.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable open-folder icon that can inherit color from surrounding UI text or icon styling.
contribution: Contributes a compact, themeable open-folder vector icon with round-stroked outline geometry for use in navigation, file browsing, or folder-related UI states.
summary_for_embedding: This is a two-path 24x24 SVG open-folder outline icon using currentColor stroke, no fill, 1.5 stroke width, and rounded caps and joins.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing an opened folder state as a reusable, color-inheriting UI icon asset.
